lines: You crouch over him. A settlement guard on his back, knifed far past what killing takes, in a pool that has not finished drying. A chapel key is still tied inside his sleeve. They did not want it.
His guard coat has been opened at the chest and the skin beneath it carved. A star, five lines, driven in point-down. Below it, cut slower and deeper, two lines: NOT HOLY. DO NOT FEAST.

You have seen wound-stars before. Every road-cult cuts something. But the rest of this is new to you. Below the star, someone took the time to carve a verdict: this one was checked, judged closed, and refused. The ones they kept were guards too. The man on the cross. The one held below. They open and eat only the ones they decide are turning holy, and they leave the merely faithful in the road with a warning so the next of them does not waste a bite. You should have a name for a cult that works this way. You do not, and that is the part that unsettles you.
You draw his coat shut over the star. It does nothing for him. You do it anyway.
